title: Guided Munitions Final Assembly Under License
entry_mechanism: Licensed final assembly under existing Saudi munitions procurement contracts via IPP conversion
executive_summary:
Saudi Arabia is one of the world's largest importers of guided munitions — Paveway laser-guided bombs, JDAM GPS-guided kits, Brimstone air-launched missiles, Storm Shadow cruise missiles, and AMRAAM air-to-air missiles. Every one of these weapons is manufactured entirely outside the Kingdom and shipped to Saudi Arabia as a complete article. Node MS-1 targets the first and most accessible step toward munitions sovereignty: licensed final assembly of guided munitions in-Kingdom, using imported components assembled by Saudi technicians to OEM specifications.

Final assembly under licence is the munitions industry's established model for transferring industrial capability to allied nations — the UK, Australia, South Korea, and Japan all manufacture US-designed munitions under Production and Support Agreements. Saudi Arabia's scale of munitions procurement ($2–4 billion annually) creates the economic mass to justify a dedicated final assembly facility, and GAMI's PSAP framework provides the regulatory structure to attract OEM partners.

The global guided munitions market was valued at $48 billion in 2024, growing at 6.8% CAGR driven by global rearmament. Saudi Arabia's addressable final assembly opportunity — capturing the assembly value-add from its existing procurement — is estimated at $300–500 million annually at steady state.

companies.name: Raytheon (RTX)
companies.rationale: Raytheon supplies Paveway, AMRAAM, and AIM-9X to Saudi Arabia under Foreign Military Sales. Raytheon has established licensed assembly programmes internationally and has significant IPP obligations in Saudi Arabia.
companies.contact: Raytheon VP Saudi Arabia Programmes → RTX International Industrial Cooperation

companies.name: MBDA
companies.rationale: MBDA supplies Brimstone, Storm Shadow, and Meteor to Saudi Arabia and has established licensed production in multiple countries. European IPP obligations support Saudi assembly investment.
companies.contact: MBDA VP Saudi Arabia — Industrial Cooperation

companies.name: Boeing Defence
companies.rationale: Boeing produces JDAM guidance kits and Paveway variants with significant Saudi Arabia sales. Boeing has production licence programmes for allied nations.
companies.contact: Boeing Defence VP International Industrial Partnerships

companies.name: Elbit Systems Munitions
companies.rationale: Elbit produces guided munitions including SkyStriker loitering munitions. Subject to Saudi-Israeli normalisation.

companies.name: Rheinmetall Waffe Munition
companies.rationale: Rheinmetall manufactures guided artillery munitions and has established production partnerships internationally. Active Saudi Arabia engagement.
companies.contact: Rheinmetall Waffe Munition VP International Programmes

partners.name: SAMI / Saudi Arabian Military Industries Munitions
partners.current: Munitions programme management; some conventional ammunition production; explosives handling safety
partners.upgrade: Establish Raytheon and MBDA licensed final assembly facility; explosives manufacturing licence from MOI; 100+ Saudi assembly technicians; ASQ9100 for munitions; first Paveway and Brimstone assembly lines
partners.timeline: 36 months to first licensed munitions delivery

partners.name: Royal Ordnance / Defence Science and Technology (Saudi)
partners.current: Some conventional munitions research and production; explosives expertise
partners.upgrade: Guided munitions component manufacturing research; Saudi explosives filling capability; quality standards for guided munitions production

partners.name: King Abdulaziz City for Science and Technology
partners.current: Defence research; materials science; some energetic materials research
partners.upgrade: Munitions propellant characterisation research; warhead design research (Phase III aspiration); support SAMI munitions quality programme

partners.name: GAPT (General Administration for Personnel Training)
partners.current: Military technical training; explosives handling training
partners.upgrade: Guided munitions assembly technician programme; Raytheon and MBDA factory training pipeline; explosives and safety qualification
